message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix J-003 in .github/workflows/ci.yml + requirements-build.lock
What: hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock, hatchling==1.30.1) installed + --no-build-isolation editable install + sync-check; no live un-hashed hatchling fetch.
Test(s): test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Show the actual diff for J-003

content.content:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index ed0873a..9e1e25c 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -32,7 +32,8 @@ jobs:
           # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -77,7 +78,8 @@ jobs:
           # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
       - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
@@ -136,6 +138,12 @@ jobs:
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
+          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
+          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -188,9 +196,10 @@ jobs:
           # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
           python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
-          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
+          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report
         if: always()
diff --git a/requirements-build.lock b/requirements-build.lock
new file mode 100644

317	def test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation():
318	    # #J-003: the editable install must use the hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock) with
319	    # --no-build-isolation, so hatchling isn't fetched live/un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned,
320	    # non-reproducible build-time code-exec window).
321	    text = _ci_text()
322	    assert "--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ." in text, "build isolation still live (#J-003)"
323	    assert "requirements-build.lock" in text
324	    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").exists()
325	    build = (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
326	    assert "hatchling==" in build and "--hash=sha256:" in build
327	    assert "pip install --no-deps -e .\n" not in text  # the old live-isolation install is gone
328	
329	
330	def test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set():
331	    # #J-006: the license gate must scan the dependency set, not entire site-packages
332	    # (--with-system pulls system tooling that can spuriously fail or mask the gate).
333	    text = _ci_text()
334	    assert "pip-licenses --format=json --with-system" not in text, (
335	        "license gate still --with-system"
336	    )
337	
338	
339	def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
340	    # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
341	    # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.
342	    import re
343	
344	    text = _ci_text()
345	    uses = re.findall(r"uses:\s*(\S+)", text)
346	    assert uses, "no actions found"
347	    for ref in uses:
348	        _, _, pin = ref.partition("@")
349	        assert re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{40}", pin), f"action {ref!r} not pinned to a commit SHA"
350	
351	
352	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
353	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.
354	    text = _ci_text()
355	    assert "cyclonedx-json" in text and "sbom" in text.lower()
356	
357	
358\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
359	def test_allowlist_parse_is_newline_agnostic_and_comment_tolerant(tmp_path):
360	    # #131/#145: the last advisory id must be honored even with no trailing newline, comments and
361	    # blank lines skipped, and ids must not word-split/glob-expand.
362	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
363	    f.write_bytes(b"# a comment\n\nGHSA-aaaa\nPYSEC-2024-last-no-newline")  # no trailing \n
364	    out = subprocess.run(
365	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
366	        capture_output=True,
367	        text=True,
368	        check=True,
369	    ).stdout
370	    assert "GHSA-aaaa" in out
371	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" in out, "final newline-less id dropped (#131)"
372	    assert "# a comment" not in out, "comment line leaked into ignores (#131)"
373	
374	    # revert-discrimination: the original loop DROPS the final newline-less id.
375	    buggy = subprocess.run(
376	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY, "_", str(f)],
377	        capture_output=True,
378	        text=True,
379	        check=True,
380	    ).stdout
381	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" not in buggy, (
382	        "buggy loop unexpectedly kept the id — test no longer discriminates"
383	    )
384	
385	
386\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
387	def test_allowlist_ids_do_not_glob_expand(tmp_path):
388	    # #145: an allowlist line is passed as a single argv element even if it contains shell glob
389	    # metacharacters (it must never expand against the working directory).
390	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
391	    f.write_text("GHSA-*\n", encoding="utf-8")
392	    (tmp_path / "GHSA-real-file").write_text("x", encoding="utf-8")
393	    out = subprocess.run(
394	        ["bash", "-c", f"cd {tmp_path} && " + _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
395	        capture_output=True,
396	        text=True,
397	        check=True,
398	    ).stdout
399	    assert "GHSA-*" in out, "glob metacharacter expanded against cwd (#145)"
400	    assert "GHSA-real-file" not in out

Now verify discrimination: revert the ci.yml change and confirm the test fails.

sed -i '' 's/pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e \.$/pip install --no-deps -e ./' .github/workflows/ci.yml
git checkout HEAD~1 -- requirements-build.lock 2>/dev/null; rm -f requirements-build.lock
python3 -m pytest tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation -q 2>&1 | tail -8

cp /tmp/ci.yml.bak .github/workflows/ci.yml && cp /tmp/build.lock.bak requirements-build.lock

git status --short

content.text:
Restored cleanly, test passes again. Let me verify the build lock is internally consistent (hatchling is a real PEP 517 backend and the deps look right).

The fix:
- ci.yml: all three editable installs changed from `pip install --no-deps -e .` to `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock` (hashed backend) followed by `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .`. This prevents a live, un-hashed hatchling fetch from PyPI at build time.
- A sync-check step regenerates the build lock via `uv pip compile` and diffs it, so it can't go stale.
- requirements-build.lock: new, fully hash-pinned (`hatchling==1.30.1` + editables + transitive deps with `--hash=sha256:`).

Test assertions are well-targeted: requires `--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .`, presence of `requirements-build.lock` reference + file + `hatchling==` + `--hash=sha256:`, and explicitly asserts the old `pip install --no-deps -e .\n` line is gone. Discrimination confirmed both directions.

input.reasoning: Read both changed files at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and the test. APPROPRIATE: ci.yml now replaces all three `pip install --no-deps -e .` invocations (jobs: core lint, real-backend mypy, and license/supply-chain) with `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock` (hashed build backend) followed by `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .`. This closes the un-hashed live hatchling fetch / build-time code-exec window. requirements-build.lock is new, uv-compiled with --generate-hashes, fully pinned (hatchling==1.30.1, editables==0.6 + transitive deps packaging/pathspec/pluggy/trove-classifiers, all with --hash=sha256). A sync-check step was added that regenerates the build lock via `uv pip compile` and diffs it, preventing staleness — idiomatic and consistent with the existing requirements-core.lock sync guard. No regression: --no-deps preserves the ADR-0006 backend-isolation guard. COMPLETE/DISCRIMINATES: test passes on HEAD; reverting the install line back to `pip install --no-deps -e .` and removing requirements-build.lock makes it FAIL with "build isolation still live (#J-003)". The test also asserts the old `pip install --no-deps -e .\n` line is absent, hatchling==/--hash present, and the lock file exists — all directly tied to the fix. Working tree restored cleanly after the experiment; test green again.

content.text: Verified. J-003 is CONFIRMED — the fix is appropriate and the named test discriminates (fails when reverted).
